memory.summary: Teammate offered to share workload, but I need to finish my own part by 13:00 as planned. With low energy and high stress, accepting extra tasks seems risky.
Teammate A urgently requested help at 15:00, but my own project progress is still low. Given my high stress and recent memory of declining offers, I must prioritize finishing my assigned tasks.
Teammate unexpectedly dumped half their work on me, increasing my load despite low energy and stress. I must now prioritize finishing my own tasks while managing this sudden burden.
I finished 8% of my project but still have less than an hour left. With low energy and high stress, I must complete my task immediately before the deadline.
My deadline passed unfinished, and now my teammate's work is also overdue. With high stress and low energy, I must address both pending tasks immediately.
My workload has increased further as my teammate added more to my load. With energy near zero and stress high, I must prioritize completing these tasks immediately.
My project progress improved slightly to 0.70 after two hours, yet I remain critically low on energy and stressed while my teammate's work is also overdue.
My teammate dumped their work on me, increasing my load to 0.23 while I remain at zero energy and high stress.
Teammate dumped work on me, increasing my load to 0.29 while I remain at zero energy and high stress.
After 1.5 hours, project progress rose from 0.70 to 0.75 despite zero energy and high stress.
Despite zero energy and high stress, I made measurable progress (0.75 to 0.81) in two hours. My teammate's previous dumping of work seems to have finally yielded results.
My teammate's dumped work has increased my load to 0.32, yet I still managed progress despite zero energy and high stress.
